scoring_notes.marker_scoring: Fraction of classical defining_markers (Calbindin/Calb1, NeuN/Rbfox3, Tbr1) found in atlas node defining_markers. All three are broadly expressed in mature DG granule cells and typically filtered from cluster-level differential markers, so zero direct hits are expected. Curator mapping notes for DG Glut clusters explicitly reference calbindin as a subclass feature, providing indirect support. Tbr1 presence in DG-PIR Ex IMN_2 clusters (0514/0515) indicates immature-stage identity. Prox1 and Neurod2 in DG Glut clusters are supporting mature-GN TF markers (not in classical list but corroborating).
scoring_notes.negative_marker_scoring: DCX, Nestin, PSA-NCAM checked against atlas defining_markers. DCX and Nestin absent from all DG Glut cluster markers (consistent with mature identity). PSA-NCAM is post-translational; intrinsically absent. No penalties applied to DG Glut clusters.
scoring_notes.nt_alignment: All DG Glut and DG-PIR Ex IMN clusters carry nt_type Glut — CONSISTENT with classical glutamatergic designation.
scoring_notes.location_alignment: All DG Glut clusters localise to UBERON:0001885 (dentate gyrus of hippocampal formation) in GCL sub-regions (DG ve, DG do, DG po) — EXACT match. DG-PIR Ex IMN_1 (0511) is SGZ — MISMATCH for mature GN.
scoring_notes.curator_cl_bonus: Atlas CL mapping to CL:2000089 (dentate gyrus granule cell) EXACT for all DG Glut clusters and their supertypes — directly corresponds to the canonical classical term for mature DG granule cell.

scoring_methodology.note: Curator CL:2000089 EXACT mapping is treated as a direct evidence signal weighted separately. Marker score is low for all nodes due to broadly-expressed mature markers being filtered from cluster-level differential lists; this is methodologically expected and does not reduce candidate credibility.

marker_supporting_evidence: Neurod2 (mature DG GC TF, not in classical list)
Glis3 (mature DG GC TF, not in classical list)
Prox1 absent (not discriminating at cluster level but present at subclass)
No Tbr1 (correctly absent = consistent with mature, not immature, identity)
score_components.location_detail: UBERON:0001885 dentate gyrus; MERFISH name_in_source DG do (dorsal DG), CCF HIP:0.98

candidates.rationale: Largest DG Glut_2 cluster (v3.size 6901 / v2.size 35349 — highest cell count across all DG Glut clusters, making it the most representative mature DG GN cluster). NT CONSISTENT. Location EXACT (dorsal DG GCL, HIP:0.98). No negative markers present. Curator EXACT CL:2000089 mapping. Lacks Tbr1 (correctly absent for mature identity). Neurod2, Glis3, St18, Mkx, Prdm5, Foxo1 are mature granule cell TF markers. Neuropeptides Cck and Pdyn are documented granule cell peptides. Score penalised only by absence of direct Calbindin/NeuN/Tbr1 symbol matches, which is methodologically expected.
caveats: Calbindin (Calb1), NeuN (Rbfox3) not listed in cluster defining_markers — these are broadly expressed and filtered from cluster-level differential expression lists; their presence at the subclass level is documented in curator mapping notes.
Gad1 listed as nt_marker (score 5.29) alongside Slc17a7 — may reflect ambient RNA or minor GABAergic contamination; not a negative for Glut identity.
No direct Tbr1 hit confirms mature (not immature) stage identity.

score_components.location_detail: UBERON:0001885 dentate gyrus; MERFISH DG do (dorsal DG), CCF HIP:0.99

candidates.rationale: Second-largest DG Glut_2 cluster (v3.size 4162, v2.size 16341). NT CONSISTENT. Location EXACT (dorsal DG GCL, HIP:0.99 — highest CCF specificity across DG Glut_2 clusters). No neuropeptides (consistent with classical mature GN profile which lacks neuropeptides). No negative markers. Curator EXACT CL:2000089. Rich set of mature DG GC TF markers (Glis3, Neurod2, Prdm5, Zkscan16, Mkx). Scoped markers Serinc2 and Plk5 provide cluster-level distinction within DG Glut_2.
caveats: Calbindin/NeuN absent from defining_markers — expected at cluster differential level.
Gad1 nt_marker signal (score 5.16) alongside Slc17a7 (score 8.47) — typical ambient RNA pattern in DG Glut clusters.
No neuropeptides — while this is consistent with the classical mature GN signature (which lists no neuropeptides), it differs from clusters 0506/0507/0508/0509/0510 which express Cck/Pdyn.

score_components.location_detail: UBERON:0001885 dentate gyrus; MERFISH DG po (posterior DG), CCF HIP:0.99

candidates.rationale: DG Glut_4 cluster (v3.size 2640, v2.size 2526). Posterior DG location (DG po) — full GCL coverage across dorsal, medial, and posterior blades collectively represented by DG Glut_2–4. NT CONSISTENT. Location EXACT (HIP:0.99). No negative markers. Curator EXACT CL:2000089. Scoped markers Clec1a and Smoc2 are spatially validated by MERFISH. Distinctive neuropeptide profile (Cck, Grp, Pdyn) suggests a topographically or functionally distinct mature granule cell subpopulation.
caveats: Calbindin/NeuN absent from defining_markers — expected.
Gad1 nt_marker present (score 5.5) — same ambient RNA consideration as other DG Glut clusters.
Three neuropeptides (Cck, Grp, Pdyn) — may reflect a specific subpopulation of mature granule cells enriched in the posterior DG blade.

score_components.location_detail: UBERON:0001885 dentate gyrus; MERFISH DG do (dorsal DG), CCF HIP:0.99

candidates.rationale: DG Glut_2 cluster (v3.size 117, v2.size 12080). NT CONSISTENT. Location EXACT (dorsal DG, HIP:0.99). No negative markers. Curator EXACT CL:2000089. Contains Prox1 as an explicit TF marker (canonical DG granule cell identity marker persisting into maturity), plus Neurod2, Glis3, Zeb2, Mkx — collectively a strong mature-GN TF signature. Single neuropeptide Cck.
caveats: Calbindin/NeuN absent from defining_markers — expected.
Slc17a7 score not noted explicitly but present as nt_marker.

score_components.location_detail: UBERON:0001885 dentate gyrus; MERFISH DG do, CCF HIP:0.96

candidates.rationale: DG Glut_3 cluster (v3.size 29, v2.size 136 — small cluster). NT CONSISTENT. Location EXACT (dorsal DG, HIP:0.96). No negative markers. Curator EXACT CL:2000089. Prox1, Neurod2, Mkx, St18 are mature GC TF markers. MERFISH-validated Egr2 spatially confirms DG dorsal granule layer localization.
caveats: Small cluster size (v3.size 29) — may represent a minor subpopulation or a technical artifact.
Cenpa (centromere protein A) as a scoped marker is unusual — may indicate transcriptional state variation.
Gad1 nt_marker present (score 6.21) — highest Gad1 signal among DG Glut_3 clusters.

score_components.location_detail: UBERON:0001885 dentate gyrus; MERFISH DG do, CCF HIP:0.96

candidates.rationale: DG Glut_3 cluster (v3.size 38, v2.size 761). NT CONSISTENT. Location EXACT (dorsal DG, HIP:0.96). No negative markers. Curator EXACT CL:2000089. Three neuropeptides including Npy (also in defining_markers as scoped — distinctive within subclass). Glis3, Neurod2, St18, Bhlhe41, Cebpb provide mature GC identity. Drd5 suggests dopamine receptor expression, potentially marking a distinct functional subpopulation.
caveats: Small cluster size (v3.size 38).
Npy as both scoped defining_marker and neuropeptide — marks this as a Npy-enriched mature granule cell subpopulation; Npy is a known modulator of adult neurogenesis.
Atf3 and Cebpb are stress-response TFs — may indicate an activity-dependent or stress-induced transcriptional state.

score_components.location_detail: MERFISH name DG ve (ventral DG); CCF broad MEA:0.74, fiber tracts:0.1 — LOW HIP fraction, indicating many cells map outside DG in CCF. Ventral DG is part of UBERON:0001885 but the high MEA fraction raises registration uncertainty.

candidates.anatomical_location_atlas: DG ve (UBERON:0001885 — ventral DG, but CCF shows MEA:0.74)
candidates.rationale: DG Glut_1 cluster (v3.size 246, v2.size 50). NT CONSISTENT. Location APPROXIMATE — MERFISH assigns cells to DG ventral (part of UBERON:0001885) but CCF broad registration shows MEA:0.74 with only a minor HIP fraction, indicating major registration ambiguity. Curator EXACT CL:2000089. Contains Prox1, Neurod2, Glis3, Lhx9, Rfx2 — strong mature GC TF panel. Scoped markers Dio3 and Tshz2 are spatially validated by MERFISH. Score reduced due to location uncertainty.
caveats: CCF broad annotation MEA:0.74 raises serious location uncertainty — most cells may not register to DG in the CCF atlas, despite MERFISH 'DG ve' label.
Ventral DG granule cells are a known functional subpopulation with distinct connectivity; this cluster may represent a bona fide but topographically distinct mature granule cell type.
Scoped markers Dio3 and Tshz2 provide cluster-level identity within DG Glut_1.

score_components.location_detail: MERFISH DG ve; CCF broad CA3sp:0.18, DG-mo:0.17, DG-po:0.17 — CCF shows DG sub-regions present but also CA3 cross-contamination

candidates.anatomical_location_atlas: DG ve (UBERON:0001885 — ventral DG, but CCF shows CA3/DG mixed)
candidates.rationale: DG Glut_1 cluster (v3.size 117, v2.size 734). NT CONSISTENT. Location APPROXIMATE — MERFISH 'DG ve' but CCF shows mixed signal across DG subregions and CA3, reducing anatomical specificity. Curator EXACT CL:2000089. Prox1, Neurod2, Glis3, Hey2 support mature GC identity. Scoped markers Dio3 and Ndst4 MERFISH-validated.
caveats: CCF scatter across CA3 subfields in addition to DG — may indicate ventral DG granule cells with mossy fiber-adjacent registration, or technical scatter.
Shares Dio3 with cluster 0502 — both are DG ve clusters in the DG Glut_1 supertype; cluster 0502 and 0503 likely represent a ventral DG mature GN population.

score_components.location_detail: MERFISH DG ve; CCF broad MEA:0.2, DG-po:0.17, CA3sp:0.11, DG-mo:0.11 — mixed CCF registration

candidates.anatomical_location_atlas: DG ve (UBERON:0001885 — ventral DG, mixed CCF)
candidates.rationale: DG Glut_1 cluster (v3.size 44, v2.size 72). NT CONSISTENT. Location APPROXIMATE — same ventral DG ambiguity as 0502/0503. Curator EXACT CL:2000089. Prox1, Glis3, Nr4a3, Egr3, Rorb are TF markers. Four neuropeptides including Penk and Cartpt — suggests a highly peptidergic mature GN subpopulation.
caveats: Smallest v3 cell count (44) among DG Glut_1 clusters.

Four neuropeptides (Grp, Penk, Cck, Cartpt) — the richest neuropeptide profile among all DG Glut clusters; Cartpt also appears as a MERFISH-validated defining_marker. This may represent a distinct peptidergic subpopulation.
Location uncertainty same as 0502/0503.

marker_supporting_evidence: Tbr1 present (classical defining_marker of mature GN — but in this immature cluster it marks the postmitotic transition, not terminal maturation)
score_components.location_detail: MERFISH DGdo,me; CCF HIP:1.0 — excellent HIP fraction but DGdo,me is inner GCL (compatible with GCL classical location); however identity is immature GN not mature GN
score_components.curator_cl_mapping: CL:9900002 (immature dentate gyrus granule neuron) EXACT — different CL term; classical target is CL:2000089

candidates.rationale: Tbr1 is listed as a classical defining_marker for the mature GN (from Hodge 2008 cascade), and it IS present in this cluster — but this reflects the postmitotic transition (immature GN stage), not terminal maturation. The curator maps this cluster EXACT to CL:9900002 (immature GN), not CL:2000089 (mature GN). Location (DGdo,me inner GCL) overlaps with mature GN territory but the cellular identity is immature. Score is low due to wrong CL mapping and intermediate NT status. Included as the closest DG-PIR Ex IMN candidate for awareness.
caveats: Tbr1 presence here marks the postmitotic transition INTO the immature GN stage (Hodge 2008), not terminal maturation. For the mature GN, Tbr1 should be downregulated relative to immature stages.
NT type listed as 'NA' — reduces NT alignment score.
Curator EXACT mapping to CL:9900002 (immature GN) directly contradicts mature GN identity.
This cluster is the immature-to-maturing transitional stage immediately UPSTREAM of the mature GN in the lineage.

excluded_nodes.reason: Located in SGZ (UBERON:0009952) — wrong location for mature GN. Expresses Eomes (type-2b progenitor TF) — incompatible with mature GN. Curator maps to CL:9900004 (type-2b progenitor) BROAD. Composite score < 0.25.

Tbr1+ marks this as immature GN stage. Curator EXACT mapping to CL:9900002 (immature GN). Igfbpl1 is an immature marker. Score below threshold for mature GN candidates. Similar profile to 0515 (ranked 10th for awareness); excluded from primary list.
Subclass spanning both immature and neuroblast types; mapped to CL:4042028 (immature neuron) BROAD. Not a candidate for mature GN.
Supertype of type-2b neuroblast cluster 0511; Eomes+ signature incompatible with mature GN.
Supertype of immature GN clusters 0514/0515; immature identity.

summary.key_finding: All 8 DG Glut clusters (0502-0510 across supertypes DG Glut_1 through DG Glut_4) are strong candidates for dg_mature_granule_neuron, uniformly carrying EXACT CL:2000089 (dentate gyrus granule cell) mapping, glutamatergic identity, and DG location. They differ in topographic sub-region (ventral DG ve for 0502-0504, dorsal DG do for 0505-0509, posterior DG po for 0510) and neuropeptide composition (Cck, Pdyn, Grp, Npy, Penk, Cartpt). The classical mature GN markers Calbindin and NeuN are absent from cluster-level differential lists across all nodes — this is expected because these proteins are broadly expressed and not cluster-discriminating. Tbr1 is correctly absent from all DG Glut clusters (confirming terminal maturation vs immature identity). DG Glut_2 clusters (0505/0506/0507) at dorsal DG with high HIP CCF fractions (0.98-0.99) are the highest-confidence candidates.
summary.lineage_note: The dg_mature_granule_neuron is the terminal endpoint of the adult neurogenesis lineage. The continuum from progenitor (0511) -> immature (0514/0515) -> mature (0502-0510) is fully represented in the atlas. The discovery is consistent with the curator evidence already present in the report_ingest YAML.
